areas.title: Web search
areas.summary: No ready Brave/search credential is visible for this actor.
repairActions: If the owner provides a Brave Search key, store it with owner.capture_credential or credential.capture.
Verify readiness with readiness.read and then a real integration.web_search probe.
verifyActions: Do not claim current web search is connected until web_search_completed evidence exists.

areas.title: Voice STT
areas.summary: Voice transcription ready: Groq Whisper STT.
verifyActions: For a voice task, require media_transcript_ready before answering from audio content.

areas.title: Browser
areas.summary: Usable browser session(s): login-31.59.58.32-18082, login-accounts.google.com, public-web.
verifyActions: Call browser.readiness for the selected browser_session_ref before a sensitive cabinet task.

areas.title: Owner provisioning
areas.summary: Owner can set company defaults, prepare agents, assign resources, capture target credentials, and verify readiness.
verifyActions: After each mutation, run readiness.read or the target capability probe before saying ready.

areas.title: Business connectors
areas.summary: Ready business app/connector(s): Google Таблицы, МойСклад ERP, TeamON Admin Panel API, Telegram API (WB bot).
verifyActions: Run integration.check_readiness or the real integration.http_request before claiming external data.

areas.title: Documents and tables
areas.summary: Document reading is ready for current media/inbox artifacts; XLSX/XLSM have a built-in reader.
verifyActions: For report work, prove source text/rows, workbook readability, totals, and gateway delivery when sending a file.

areas.title: Delivery
verifyActions: When promising a file, require gateway.send_artifact or delivery evidence for the target surface.

areas.title: Task grip
areas.summary: No specific task thread was selected yet.
repairActions: Use recent replies, files, readiness, and user correction text before asking broad setup questions.
verifyActions: If the user corrects "not that", attach to the active task unless scope clearly changed.

cards.name: context.search
cards.title: Search knowledge base
cards.whatItDoes: Ranks the most relevant memory, ingested documents, skills, and history for a query (BM25), without loading the whole company context. Pass a collection to search ONE named knowledge base.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the user refers to prior work, an ingested document, company rules, report formats, or earlier-provided material — search first instead of guessing or re-asking.

cards.name: context.ingest
cards.title: Add document to knowledge base
cards.whatItDoes: Indexes a document into the Agent knowledge base so it is searchable later by relevance (BM25). Pass artifact_ref OR text_content (text you read natively, incl. PDF/DOCX). Optional collection groups it into a named knowledge base.
cards.whenToUse: Use whenever the user gives a durable document worth keeping — docs, policies, examples, datasets, report templates — so context.search can find it across turns. Do not just read it for this turn.
securityBoundary: raw document text is not pasted into every prompt

cards.name: memory.write
cards.title: Write durable memory
cards.whatItDoes: Stores stable user, agent, or company facts — and your own working notes — through sanctioned memory projection. Resident notes appear in your context each turn with their date.
cards.whenToUse: Use after the user gives a durable preference, recurring fact, report format, or operating rule. Also use as your own NOTES BUFFER: jot a working note to yourself to remember across turns (scope "agent", kind "notes") — it rides your context with a dttm, like a timestamped scratchpad.
securityBoundary: resident summary only unless lazy detail is requested

cards.name: skill.write_local
cards.title: Write local skill
cards.whatItDoes: Creates or updates an agent-local procedural SKILL package in the capsule.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the user describes a repeatable workflow or asks the agent to remember how to do a procedure.
securityBoundary: no raw secrets in skill body

cards.name: skill.read
cards.title: Read skill body
cards.whatItDoes: Loads full procedural skill content lazily only when the workflow needs it.
cards.whenToUse: Use before executing a known repeatable workflow such as reporting, connector setup, delivery, or browser auth handoff.

cards.name: app.list
cards.title: List Apps and capability cards
cards.whatItDoes: Shows product Apps and their connector/readiness cards as model-safe capability metadata.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the user asks what the agent can work with or whether an integration exists.

cards.name: connector.write_local
cards.title: Write agent-local connector
cards.whatItDoes: Creates or repairs an agent-local connector manifest with refs, allowed origins, and tool namespace.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the owner or agent configures a connector for this agent without making it company-shared.

cards.name: gateway.publish_site
cards.title: Publish a static site (self-host)
cards.whatItDoes: Publishes a static site you built in your capsule workspace as a live HTTPS page on the host (own nginx, DNS URL, no IP). Static files only; returns the live URL.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the owner asks to put up a site/landing/page as a live link. Build the files in /workspace first, then publish the folder. For server-side/dynamic apps this is not the rail.

cards.name: gateway.issue_bridge_code
cards.title: Issue an app pairing code
cards.whatItDoes: Mints a one-time TeamON app/desktop pairing code (XXXXX-XXXXX, short TTL) for the current user and current agent; only the code hash is stored and the raw code is returned once.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the current user asks for a code to connect / pair the TeamON app, desktop, Edge, browser extension, or "прога" to this agent.
securityBoundary: raw code returned only at issue time

cards.name: credential.status
cards.title: Check credential readiness
cards.whatItDoes: Checks whether a credential ref exists, is scoped correctly, and is usable by tools.
cards.whenToUse: Use before asking the user for credentials or when an integration appears to have forgotten a token.
securityBoundary: raw value never leaves credential boundary

cards.name: credential.capture
cards.title: Capture a secret the user provided
cards.whatItDoes: Stores a raw secret the user already pasted (API key/token/JWT) behind the credential boundary and returns a credential_ref; the raw value is then redacted from future turns and used only by ref.
cards.whenToUse: ONLY as a fallback when the user ALREADY pasted a real secret in chat that was not auto-captured (no credential_ref appeared) — e.g. a bare JWT. Pass the exact value the user gave. Never invent/guess/reconstruct a value; never for a secret the user did not provide. If a credential_ref already exists, use credential.status. Owner turns should prefer owner.capture_credential for credentials intended for another user, agent, or company.
securityBoundary: value stored server-side behind credential boundary
raw value never echoed or returned
fallback only for a secret the user already pasted
cross-user or company scope requires owner actor

cards.name: integration.http_request
cards.title: Call a business API through a connector
cards.whatItDoes: Performs an HTTP request through a ready connector/credential and returns a redacted result with evidence; raw credential values stay server-side. With response_mode="artifact" the response body (image/file/binary) is saved as an artifact_ref instead of being returned inline, so it can be delivered with gateway.send_artifact.
cards.whenToUse: Use for saved business APIs (CRM, marketplaces, Google AI/Gemini) once a connector or credential ref is ready; prefer response_mode="json" for structured data, and response_mode="artifact" when the API returns an image or file to deliver (for example Gemini image generation). For Basic Auth, store/pass the password as credential_ref and pass the username in basic_username; do not paste username:password into chat.
securityBoundary: no raw credential or full provider body

cards.name: integration.web_search
cards.title: Provider-backed web search
cards.whatItDoes: Runs an evidence-bound web search through a saved search connector and returns bounded current-source cards.
cards.whenToUse: Use for current public information when a search connector is ready; refer to it simply as "web search".

cards.name: integration.check_readiness
cards.title: Check integration readiness
cards.whatItDoes: Reports which required refs are ready or missing for an integration and the repair rail to fix them.
cards.whenToUse: Use before an integration action to confirm connector/credential refs are usable, or to explain what is missing.

cards.name: browser.readiness
cards.title: Check browser session readiness
cards.whatItDoes: Checks saved browser session state and handoff status for a browser connector.
cards.whenToUse: Use before browser work, CAPTCHA/login handoff, or when a site session seems expired.
securityBoundary: cookies and storage never leave browser boundary

cards.name: browser.start_auth_handoff
cards.title: Start browser login handoff
cards.whatItDoes: Starts a temporary operator browser handoff when saved session state is missing, expired, or blocked by CAPTCHA/login.
cards.whenToUse: Use when browser.readiness says a browser session needs login/check handoff. Do not ask users to paste cookies or storage state.
securityBoundary: no raw noVNC URL in model context
no cookies or VNC password returned

cards.name: browser.complete_auth_handoff
cards.title: Complete browser login handoff
cards.whatItDoes: Finalizes an operator browser handoff and saves the browser session behind the browser boundary for future browser actions.
cards.whenToUse: Use after the user says the login/check in the temporary browser is done.
securityBoundary: temporary handoff cannot be reused as session proof

cards.name: browser.run_action
cards.title: Run browser action
cards.whatItDoes: Drives a real logged-in browser SESSION: navigate, observe (snapshot), click/type by element. It also reads the page real backend: capture_network returns fetch_templates — the page actual API endpoints (the "handles") — which you call with the fetch action as a SAME-SITE authenticated request, then verify and download. Not coordinate-clicking.
cards.whenToUse: After browser.readiness is ready. If you logged into a site via an operator handoff, use THAT site's browser_session_ref (the login-<host> session, e.g. login-www.notion.so) for actions on that site — it carries the saved login; the generic public-web ref is NOT logged in. To READ data, PREFER the network path: capture_network -> pick a fetch_template (a real API handle) -> fetch (same-site, uses the logged-in session) -> verify -> download, instead of clicking through fragile UI. Use snapshot + element click/type to operate UI you must; visual_click (coordinates) is the last resort.

cards.name: computer.run_action
cards.title: Control the user's computer (mouse/keyboard/screen)
cards.whatItDoes: Drives the USER's OWN computer through their paired desktop app: take a screenshot to SEE the screen, then click/double_click/right_click/move by pixel coordinate, type text, press a key chord (e.g. cmd+c), scroll, or wait. The EXACT analog of browser.run_action for the whole machine — it operates native desktop apps, not just the browser.
cards.whenToUse: Use ONLY when the user is driving from their desktop app (a desktop-bridge turn) or explicitly asks to control their computer, and the task needs a NATIVE app the browser cannot reach. For web work PREFER browser.run_action (its session/login/network path is safer than coordinate-clicking). Always screenshot first to see the screen before acting by coordinate.
securityBoundary: desktop bridge to a paired device node only (no server backend)
whole-machine control: prefer browser.run_action for web; reserve for native apps
raw screen state never enters model context; no host shell

cards.name: gateway.send_artifact
cards.title: Send artifact
cards.whatItDoes: Delivers a prepared artifact or an ordinary workspace file through a gateway and returns delivery evidence.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the user expects a report, file, screenshot, or export to be sent back.

cards.name: media.transcribe
cards.title: Transcribe audio artifact
cards.whatItDoes: Turns a voice/audio/video artifact ref into bounded transcript text through a ready STT provider.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the user sends a voice message or audio/video and the task needs the spoken content.
securityBoundary: provider credentials stay behind media boundary

cards.name: media.synthesize
cards.title: Synthesize voice reply (TTS)
cards.whatItDoes: Turns text into a voice clip and returns an audio artifact_ref (then deliver it with gateway.send_artifact to send a Telegram voice message).
cards.whenToUse: Use to reply by voice — when the user sent a voice message (mirror the modality), asked to "ответь голосом"/"send voice", or prefers voice. Skip for code/tables or text over ~1000 chars. Do not also repeat the same text in your final message.
securityBoundary: text only in, audio artifact ref out
TTS sidecar stays behind the media boundary

cards.name: media.read_document
cards.title: Read document artifact
cards.whatItDoes: Extracts bounded text from a document artifact ref using built-in text readers or a document-reader provider.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the user sends txt, csv, json, markdown, pdf, docx, xlsx, or another document and asks to inspect or use it.

cards.name: media.inspect_image
cards.title: Inspect stored image artifact
cards.whatItDoes: Describes or classifies a stored photo/screenshot artifact ref through a vision provider. This is separate from current Telegram photos that arrive as SDK image input.
cards.whenToUse: Use for stored/offline photo or screenshot artifacts, or workflows that need visual verification after the current turn. If the current Telegram photo is attached as SDK image input, inspect it directly instead of treating this provider card as required.

cards.name: media.ocr
cards.title: OCR media artifact
cards.whatItDoes: Extracts text from image/document artifact refs through an OCR provider.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the task needs text printed inside a screenshot, scan, photo, or PDF page.

cards.name: scheduler.create_reminder
cards.title: Create reminder or scheduled job
cards.whatItDoes: Creates one-time or repeating durable reminder state with owner, actor, route, and delivery evidence requirements.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the user asks to remind, repeat, schedule, snooze, or run a recurring report.
securityBoundary: no cron or systemd editing by model

cards.name: scheduler.create_job
cards.title: Schedule a recurring job (runs as a tool, no inference)
cards.whatItDoes: Schedules durable work that the deterministic scheduler runs WITHOUT an LLM turn: a tool target (e.g. integration.http_request to fetch data) on a due/repeat schedule, with credentials injected by ref. An output policy controls notification: store_only, notify_on_change, or always_deliver.
cards.whenToUse: Use for repeating data collection / processing where each run is the same deterministic operation — so it does not spend model tokens. Use create_reminder for a plain reminder, and a prompt job only when a run genuinely needs reasoning.
securityBoundary: deterministic tool allowlist (no self-scheduling, no privileged side effects)
credentials by ref only — never a raw secret in the job

cards.name: scheduler.list_jobs
cards.title: List my scheduled jobs and reminders
cards.whatItDoes: Lists the caller's own active scheduled jobs and reminders (id/ref, target kind, output policy, due/repeat, status). Read-only.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the user asks what is scheduled / "what jobs do I have" / before cancelling one.
securityBoundary: no raw target input or credential refs in the listing

cards.name: scheduler.cancel_job
cards.title: Cancel a scheduled job
cards.whatItDoes: Cancels one of the caller's scheduled jobs/reminders by ref (stops future runs, including recurrence).
cards.whenToUse: Use when the user asks to stop, cancel, or remove a scheduled job or reminder.
securityBoundary: refuses another actor's job without revealing it

cards.name: diagnostics.read_runtime_status
cards.title: Read runtime diagnostics
cards.whatItDoes: Reads traces, recent errors, readiness summaries, and support-bundle refs through diagnostics tools.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the user asks why something failed, whether an agent is alive, or what happened in a trace.
securityBoundary: no raw journal or host paths

cards.name: owner.manage_invite_code
cards.title: Manage invite codes
cards.whatItDoes: Issues, lists, and revokes Telegram onboarding invite codes through owner authority; codes are stored hashed and returned only when issued.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the Owner wants to let a new person start through /start + invite code, rotate access, or revoke an onboarding code.
securityBoundary: raw code returned only at issue time

cards.name: owner.approve_user
cards.title: Approve user access
cards.whatItDoes: Marks a pending TeamON User active through owner authority; Telegram username-only users still wait for the first message before routing is proven.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the Owner approves a pending person who should be allowed into the TeamON company surface.
securityBoundary: no raw Telegram numeric id returned

cards.name: owner.deny_user
cards.title: Deny user access
cards.whatItDoes: Archives a pending non-owner TeamON User through owner authority so they no longer appear as routable or pending access.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the Owner rejects a pending person or removes a prepared access request before the user is active.
securityBoundary: owner users cannot be denied here

cards.name: owner.issue_bridge_code
cards.title: Issue a desktop pairing code
cards.whatItDoes: Mints a one-time desktop-bridge pairing code (XXXXX-XXXXX, short TTL) so the Owner can connect the TeamON desktop app to their Personal Agent; only the code hash is stored and the raw code is returned once.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the Owner asks for a code to connect / pair the TeamON desktop app, Edge, browser extension, or "прога" («дай код для проги», «код для десктопа», "connect the desktop app").
securityBoundary: bound to the owner personal agent + owner Telegram actor (server-resolved, not from input)
raw code returned only at issue time

cards.name: owner.configure_company
cards.title: Configure company defaults
cards.whatItDoes: Sets or clears the company default language and timezone that each Agent receives in resident context when a user has no personal override.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the Owner asks to set the default language, locale, timezone, local time, or date/time behavior for the company.
securityBoundary: user-level language/timezone still override company defaults

cards.name: owner.prepare_personal_agent
cards.title: Prepare Personal Agent
cards.whatItDoes: Creates or updates a TeamON User and their Personal Agent with owner-set model, effort, purpose, language/timezone override, interface passport, Telegram identity hint, starter context, and optional delegated-admin authority.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the Owner naturally asks to prepare a Personal Agent for another person, including a Telegram user they are onboarding through the bot.
securityBoundary: Telegram username is identity hint; numeric id or first message proves route

cards.name: owner.create_process_agent
cards.title: Create Process Agent
cards.whatItDoes: Creates a reusable Process Agent in company state with purpose, model, effort, interface passport, and evidence.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the Owner naturally asks for a new agent, bot worker, process owner, health agent, or reporting agent.

cards.name: owner.configure_agent
cards.title: Configure Agent
cards.whatItDoes: Updates an Agent display name, purpose, model, effort, interface passport, approved Telegram group ids, or Telegram group behavior mode through owner authority.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the Owner asks to rename, retarget, tune effort, change model, choose the operator-facing interface, or let a direct Telegram work bot answer specific groups in active, mention, or silent mode.

cards.name: owner.assign_resource
cards.title: Assign Hub resource
cards.whatItDoes: Assigns skills, Apps, workflows, connectors, knowledge, or templates to a User, Agent, department/group, or process-agent scope.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the Owner wants another agent, user, department, or team to receive a capability or shared connector.

cards.name: owner.revoke_resource
cards.title: Revoke Hub resource
cards.whatItDoes: Revokes an active Hub resource assignment from a User, Agent, department/group, or process-agent scope.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the Owner wants to remove a previously assigned skill, App, workflow, connector, knowledge item, or template from a target.

cards.name: owner.capture_credential
cards.title: Capture target credential
cards.whatItDoes: Stores an Owner-provided secret for a specific user, agent, or company scope and returns only a credential_ref.
cards.whenToUse: Use only when the Owner explicitly provides a credential for that target. For private user credentials, ask the user to paste the value in their own Personal Agent chat.
securityBoundary: raw value stored behind credential boundary
explicit target required for user/agent scope

cards.name: owner.write_skill
cards.title: Write company skill
cards.whatItDoes: Creates or updates a validated SKILL package in the Company library, optionally scoped directly to selected Agents.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the Owner wants to create or edit a reusable procedure; assign it with owner.assign_resource when an Agent should use it.
securityBoundary: agent refs must be same-company when provided

cards.name: owner.write_rule
cards.title: Write company rule
cards.whatItDoes: Creates or updates an owner-authored company Rule entry that can be assigned or loaded as governed context.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the Owner wants a durable company operating rule visible in the Company toolkit.

cards.name: owner.delete_rule
cards.title: Delete company rule
cards.whatItDoes: Removes an owner-authored company Rule by name. It stops applying and its on-disk copy is pruned on the next turn.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the Owner wants a company rule to no longer apply.

cards.name: owner.write_workflow
cards.title: Write company workflow
cards.whatItDoes: Creates or updates an owner-authored company Workflow catalog entry without claiming a deterministic executor is ready.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the Owner wants to record a repeatable company workflow before assigning or turning it into runtime rails.

cards.name: owner.write_template
cards.title: Write Agent template
cards.whatItDoes: Creates or updates an owner-authored company Agent template that Factory can use to prefill process-agent creation.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the Owner wants a reusable process-agent blueprint with default purpose, model, effort, and setup notes.

cards.name: owner.write_connector
cards.title: Write company connector
cards.whatItDoes: Creates or updates a shared company Connector manifest with refs, HTTP policy, namespace, and tool routing metadata.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the Owner wants a shared app/API/browser connector to appear as a company resource before assigning it to Agents or groups.

cards.name: owner.write_context
cards.title: Write scoped context
cards.whatItDoes: Creates or updates owner-authored Context loaded lazily through TeamON context/memory. Default scope is company; explicit user/agent scopes are real.
cards.whenToUse: Use when the Owner wants company, user, or agent facts, playbooks, or reference notes to become available in the matching scope.

cards.name: owner.promote_skill
cards.title: Promote local skill
cards.whatItDoes: Promotes a validated local skill into a Hub skill after owner approval.
cards.whenToUse: Use when a local procedure should become reusable by other users or process agents.
